---
title: "Fullington_diversity_analysis"
author: "Smriti"
date: "2025-06-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
#Install packages
```{r}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, dplyr, readr, codyn, lme4, lmerTest, ggrepel, tictoc, ggpubr, nlme)

```
#data upload
```{r}
df_all_with_slope_aspect <- read.csv("../data/df_all_with_slope_aspect.csv")

```
#Community structure (richness, evenness, dominance)
```{r}
colnames(df_all_with_slope_aspect)
length(unique(df_all_with_slope_aspect$Quadrat))

#Averaging by cluster, quadrat, and species
df_all.avg.rac <- df_all_with_slope_aspect %>%  dplyr::group_by(Clusters_id, Quadrat, Species) %>% dplyr::summarize(stem_count=n()) %>%  mutate(rank = rank(desc(stem_count))) %>% filter(Species!="Corner")


# Identify quadrats with multiple clusters
quadrat_with_one_cluster <- df_all.avg.rac %>%
  group_by(Quadrat) %>%                   
  summarise(n_clusters = n_distinct(Clusters_id)) %>% 
  filter(n_clusters == 1) 

# Filter the original dataframe to keep only these quadrats
df_one_cluster <- df_all.avg.rac %>%
  filter(Quadrat %in% quadrat_with_one_cluster$Quadrat)

colnames(df_one_cluster)

df_one_cluster$stem_count=as.numeric(df_one_cluster$stem_count)
df_one_cluster$Quadrat=as.character(df_one_cluster$Quadrat)


###community structure
Richness_evenness <- community_structure(
df_one_cluster,
time.var = NULL,
abundance.var = "stem_count",
replicate.var = "Quadrat",
metric = c("Evar", "SimpsonEvenness", "EQ")
)

#add land_use info
df_quadrat_info <- df_one_cluster %>% dplyr::select(Quadrat, Clusters_id)
Richness_evenness_cluster <- merge(Richness_evenness, df_quadrat_info, by="Quadrat", all.x=FALSE) %>% unique() %>%  drop_na(Evar, Clusters_id, Quadrat)

# Model for richness
model_richness <- lme(richness ~ Clusters_id,
                      random = ~1 | Quadrat,
                      data = Richness_evenness_cluster)
summary(model_richness)
anova(model_richness)
##Kruskal-Wallis richness
kruskal.test(richness ~ Clusters_id, data = Richness_evenness_cluster)

pairwise.wilcox.test(Richness_evenness_cluster$richness, Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_id, p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert cluster to factor 
Richness_evenness_cluster$Cluster <- factor(Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_id,
                                   levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                                   labels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3"))

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("1", "2"), c("1", "3"), c("2", "3"))

# Plot
richness_cluster_fig <- ggplot(Richness_evenness_cluster, aes(x = as.factor(Clusters_id), y = richness)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "slateblue", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.4, size = 2,alpha = 0.7, color= "black")  +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", size = 6, bracket.size = 1.2)  + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 22))+
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Species Richness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )



# Model for evenness
model_evenness <- lme(Evar ~ Clusters_id,
                      random = ~1 | Quadrat,
                      data = Richness_evenness_cluster)

summary(model_evenness)
anova(model_evenness)

##Kruskal-Wallis evenness
kruskal.test(Evar ~ Clusters_id, data = Richness_evenness_cluster)

pairwise.wilcox.test(Richness_evenness_cluster$Evar, Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_id, p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert cluster to factor with informative labels
Richness_evenness_cluster$Cluster <- factor(Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_id,
                                   levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                                   labels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3"))

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("1", "2"), c("1", "3"), c("2", "3"))

# Plot
evenness_cluster_fig <- ggplot(Richness_evenness_cluster, aes(x = as.factor(Clusters_id), y = Evar)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "slateblue", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, size = 2, color = "black")  +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", size = 6, bracket.size = 1.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Species Evenness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

##combined figure
combined_diversity_fig <- ggarrange(
  richness_cluster_fig, evenness_cluster_fig,
  labels = c("A)", "B)",
  ncol = 2, nrow = 1,
  align = "v",
  heights = c(1, 1) 
))

ggsave("./output/figS5.png", combined_diversity_fig, dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

```
#Stem count and basal area grouped by species and cluster number

```{r}
colnames(df_all_with_slope_aspect)

#graph for species*stem count per cluster
df_all.avg <- df_all_with_slope_aspect %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Clusters_id, ScientificName) %>% 
  dplyr::summarize(stem_count = n()) %>% 
  filter(ScientificName != "" & ScientificName != "ABCDE ABCDE" & ScientificName != "UNKNOWN UNKNOWN")
df_all.avg$Clusters_id=as.factor(df_all.avg$Clusters_id)



# Filter the top four species by stem count
top_species_unique <- df_all.avg %>%
  arrange(desc(stem_count)) %>%
  slice(1:2)  

# plot
species_stem <- ggplot(df_all.avg, aes(x = ScientificName, y = stem_count, group = Clusters_id, color = Clusters_id)) + 
  geom_line(size = 1.2) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1F77B4","#FF7F0F","#2CA02C")) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",  
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.5),  
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1, colour = "black"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"), 
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),axis.ticks.x = element_line(size = 0.9, color = "black")
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "Species Name",
    y = "Stem Count",
    color = "Cluster") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(df_all.avg$ScientificName)) +
  geom_text_repel(data = top_species_unique, aes(label = paste0("italic('", ScientificName, "')")), 
                  parse = TRUE,  
                  size = 6, color = "black", 
                  nudge_x = 1.5, nudge_y = 400, 
                  box.padding = 0.2, point.padding = 0.2, 
                  segment.color = "grey50", 
                  segment.size = 0.80,
                  segment.curvature = 0,
                  direction = "y",  
                  hjust = 0)


species_stem
ggsave("./output/figS4.png", species_stem, dpi = 300, height = 7, width = 14, units = "in")


#####################################################basal area
df_all.avg.dbh <- df_all_with_slope_aspect %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Clusters_id, ScientificName) %>% 
  dplyr::summarize(DBH=pi*sum(DBH.x^2)/40000) %>% 
  filter(ScientificName != "" & ScientificName != "ABCDE ABCDE" & ScientificName != "UNKNOWN UNKNOWN")

df_all.avg.dbh$Clusters_id=as.factor(df_all.avg.dbh$Clusters_id)

# Filter the top four species by stem count
top_species_unique_dbh <- df_all.avg.dbh %>%
  arrange(desc(DBH)) %>%
  slice(1:2)  

#plot
species_dbh <- ggplot(df_all.avg.dbh, aes(x = ScientificName, y = DBH, group = Clusters_id, color = Clusters_id)) + 
  geom_line(size = 1.2) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1F77B4","#FF7F0F","#2CA02C")) +  
    theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",  
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.5), 
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1, colour = "black"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),  
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white")
  ) +
    scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(df_all.avg.dbh$ScientificName)) +  
		labs(
    x = "Species Name",
    y = expression(paste("Basal Area ",(m^2))),
    color = "Cluster #")+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(df_all.avg$ScientificName)) +
  geom_text_repel(data = top_species_unique_dbh, aes(label = paste0("italic('", ScientificName, "')")), 
                  parse = TRUE, 
                  size = 6, color = "black", 
                  nudge_x = 1.5, nudge_y = 50,
                  box.padding = 0.2, point.padding = 0.2, 
                  segment.color = "grey50", 
                  segment.size = 0.80,  
                  segment.curvature = 0,
                  direction = "y",  
                  hjust = 0)

species_dbh
ggsave("./output/fig4c.png", species_dbh, dpi = 300, height = 7, width = 14, units = "in")

```
